summary: Matched HSD_Randi in src/sysdolphin/baselib/random.c by replacing the handwritten assembly implementation with C. The PR introduced a small inline helper, HSD_Rand_inline, to express the shared random-state update and high-16-bit extraction pattern, then implemented HSD_Randi as `(max_val * HSD_Rand_inline()) / 0x10000`. It also adjusted nearby HSD_Randf non-matching code to use the correct denominator symbol in the retained NON_MATCHING path. There was no PR body or review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Removed the inline assembly version of `HSD_Randi(s32 max_val)` and replaced it with a C implementation.
Added `inline s32 HSD_Rand_inline(void)` to update `*lbl_804D5F94` using the same linear congruential generator constants as `HSD_Rand` and return the upper 16 bits.

Implemented `HSD_Randi` as `return (max_val * HSD_Rand_inline()) / 0x10000;`, matching the assembly sequence that multiplied by the random high word and divided by 65536 via signed shift/addze behavior.
Kept `HSD_Randf` under `#ifdef NON_MATCHING` / asm fallback, but changed its non-matching denominator from `lbl_804DE798` to `lbl_804DE790` in the shown diff.
Removed `#pragma force_active on` near the random forget-memory section while retaining `#pragma push` and `#pragma peephole on` around the matched C.
smart_moves: Factored the repeated RNG state update into an inline helper so the compiler could emit the same instruction pattern expected by `HSD_Randi` without duplicating complex expression code directly in the function.
Used the literal divisor `0x10000` rather than a float/global denominator, reflecting the integer scaling performed by the original assembly.
Preserved the existing asm fallback for `HSD_Randf` instead of overclaiming that the floating-point random function was matched in the same PR.
Leveraged an inline helper to make the source-level intent clearer while still guiding code generation for matching.
decomp_lessons: For integer random scaling functions, the original assembly may implement division by 65536 as a signed shift plus addze sequence; writing `/ 0x10000` can be the C expression that matches it.
When two functions share an LCG update, introducing an inline helper can help both readability and instruction selection, especially when a callee call would not match.
Do not assume float denominator symbols used by nearby functions are appropriate for integer random functions; `HSD_Randi` needed integer scaling by `0x10000`.
Small matching PRs can remove substantial inline assembly by identifying the compact mathematical expression behind a multiply/shift sequence.
naming_conventions: Used the existing HSD/baselib function prefix convention: `HSD_Rand`, `HSD_Randf`, `HSD_Randi`, `_HSD_RandForgetMemory`.
Named the helper `HSD_Rand_inline`, explicitly indicating that it is intended to inline the shared RNG update/extract behavior.
Kept the existing parameter name `max_val` for the upper-bound argument to `HSD_Randi`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated the assembly LCG constants into C: multiply by `214013` / `0x343FD` and add `2531011` / `0x269EC3`.
Replaced the assembly multiply and signed divide-by-65536 sequence with `(max_val * HSD_Rand_inline()) / 0x10000`.
Used an `inline` helper to avoid an actual function call and to encourage the compiler to emit the expected in-place RNG update sequence.
Kept `#pragma peephole on` in the region containing the new C implementation, which may have been relevant to the emitted instruction pattern.
follow_up_queries: Check whether `HSD_Randf` was matched in a later PR and whether the denominator symbol change to `lbl_804DE790` was part of that effort.
Search for other baselib RNG functions that can reuse the `HSD_Rand_inline` pattern or the `/ 0x10000` integer scaling tactic.
Verify whether removing `#pragma force_active on` affected symbol retention or was simply unnecessary in this region.
